title: lippia ekmanii
intro: Lippia ekmanii is a species of flowering plant in the family Verbenaceae, commonly known as the verbena family. It is a perennial herb native to the Andean region of South America, traditionally used for its medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, which are believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and soothing digestive problems. In herbal preparations, Lippia ekmanii can be consumed as a tea, made by infusing the leaves in hot water, or used as a decoction to create a medicinal infusion.
